## Frontend

- All HTML files should use semantic tags appropriately (e.g., `<header>`, `<footer>`, `<article>`, `<section>`).
- Use existing components and styles from the project's design system or UI library.
- Ensure responsive design principles are applied to make the application usable on various devices and screen sizes.
- Ensure all Angular signals are created as `readonly`.
- Always use modern Angular features and best practices, avoiding deprecated APIs. Use Angular Signals for state management where applicable.
- In HTML, use the `@if` and `@for` directives for conditional rendering and loops instead of traditional Angular structural directives like `*ngIf` and `*ngFor`.

## Backend

- Use consistent naming conventions for files and directories (e.g., kebab-case for filenames).
- Write modular and reusable code by breaking down functionalities into smaller functions or classes.
- Include error handling and logging to facilitate debugging and maintenance.
- Ensure proper documentation for functions, classes, and modules to enhance code readability. Dont create any readme files here.
- Follow security best practices, such as input validation and sanitization, to protect against common vulnerabilities
- Create only LF line endings for all files.
- Follow the code structure.
